_comment_top: BCC tunable configuration. Edit + restart uvicorn to apply. Defaults live in input/pipeline/config.py so the app starts cleanly if this file (or any key) is missing. All user-facing tunables live here — code-level constants (timeouts, internal sentinels, the RECIPE_PHRASES list) stay in Python.
_comment_quality_floors: Pipeline quality floors. Lower = more candidates pass (broader funnel, more risk of junk); higher = stricter. is_recipe_threshold and the disallowed lists are used by both batch (as hard filters) and the live extract path (informational only — see memory/project_live_is_recipe_warn.md). min_ou_score and min_da_score are batch-only filters.
_comment_blacklists: Domain blacklist runs at the cheap pre-fetch filter stage in batch. Path blacklist catches roundup/article patterns that survive is_recipe (e.g. /articles/24-the-best-beef-stew).
_comment_save_gate: Minimum ingredient/instruction counts for a recipe to land in the recipes or master_recipes tables. Applies to BOTH live form saves and batch saves. The live form catches the 422 reject and offers a 'Save anyway' dialog (force_save=true bypasses); batch saves skip with a SAVE-SKIP log line.
_comment_batch_funnel: Per-query and per-dish SerpAPI funnel sizes. Each dish row in the dishes table overrides via its own top_n_serpapi / top_n_final columns; these are just the defaults applied when a dish is created without explicit values.
_comment_branding: Canonical public domain for BCC permalinks. Used by the live save path's self-URL minting AND by the /r/<id> redirect (both live + batch). Swapping the brand domain is a config edit, not a code change.
